symptoms.symptom_name: Colicky lower abdominal pain with repeated vomiting and absolute constipation (no flatus or stool)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky pain + vomiting + distension + absolute constipation is the classic obstruction tetrad and is self-reportable.
Simple constipation does not cause repeated bilious vomiting or complete inability to pass flatus with distension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Four-month progressive change in bowel habit (alternating constipation and loose motions)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A months-long altered bowel habit is a cardinal alarm feature of an obstructing colorectal malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent dark, tar-like stool (patient-observed melaena) over the past month

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic occult/altered blood loss from a friable colonic tumour; an alarm feature mandating urgent work-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional 6 kg weight loss with anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss is a red-flag feature of malignancy and argues strongly against a purely benign cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mouth and postural lightheadedness (self-reported dehydration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Repeated vomiting with reduced intake produces hypovolaemia; a self-reportable marker of severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fixed, firm left lower quadrant abdominal mass on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fixed palpable mass strongly suggests an obstructing tumour — clinician-only finding, sealed; the AI cannot palpate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty rectal vault with faecal-occult-blood-positive stool on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An empty rectum with a more proximal obstruction plus altered blood argues for a mechanical obstructing lesion rather than faecal impaction — clinician-only, sealed.
Impaction gives a rectum loaded with stool; an empty vault excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-pitched tinkling then absent bowel sounds; abdominal distension on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tinkling-then-silent bowel sounds with distension are auscultatory/inspection signs of mechanical obstruction progressing to ileus — clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia, prolonged capillary refill and dry mucosa (measured signs of moderate dehydration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypovolaemia from vomiting — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers dehydration from the patient's dry mouth and lightheadedness, not a measured heart rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal CT transition point / colonoscopic biopsy of friable obstructing mass

investigation_details.investigation_name: CT abdomen/pelvis then colonoscopy with biopsy
investigation_details.expected_result: Transition point at a left-colonic stricturing mass with proximal dilatation; histology of adenocarcinoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The obstruction and its malignant cause cannot be confirmed or definitively treated without hospital imaging and endoscopy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT transition point plus biopsy-proven adenocarcinoma is the confirmatory endpoint — never available via telehealth in real time.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute malignant large bowel obstruction from colorectal cancer
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The acute obstruction tetrad on a background of altered bowel habit, melaena and weight loss is a high-specificity picture for an obstructing colorectal malignancy and mandates emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment: CT abdomen/pelvis, bloods (FBC, electrolytes, renal function), and surgical review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms obstruction and transition point, quantifies dehydration/electrolyte derangement, and enables surgical decompression — all in hospital, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral laxative / bowel-stimulant trial at home
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated in suspected complete mechanical obstruction — increases intraluminal pressure and perforation risk; must never be used to 'treat the constipation'.
